title: Before implementing, briefly analyze: which files need changes, potential breaking changes, edge cases. Then implement the task. Task: Review failed: review score 3/10 (min 7): If score < 7, list specific issues to fix (max 5 items).
summary_text: Updated the review-fix handoff so low-score reviews now carry the actual findings instead of the prompt text. In [`canon-v3-verifier.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-verifier.sh#L29), I added structured extraction for numbered/bulleted review findings, capped at 5 items, and changed `pending-fix.md` writing to use a multiline `task: |` block when needed. I also stopped masking `codex exec` failures in the review path, so infra failures are no longer misclassified as low scores. In [`canon-v3-coder.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-coder.sh#L27) and [`canon-v3-sim-coder.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/scripts/canon-v3-sim-coder.sh#L25), I made `parse_fix` backward-compatible: it still accepts old single-line `task:` entries and now also preserves multiline issue lists. Breaking-change risk is limited to the queue-file contract, and that’s mitigated by keeping both formats supported. Covered edge cases: reviewer output with numbered lists, bullet lists, fallback keyword lines, and older queue files without block syntax. Verified with `bash -n` on all three scripts plus manual smoke checks for issue extraction and multiline queue parsing.
